positive.prompt: Inspect this repository and create a self-contained HTML system architecture diagram that distinguishes verified components, inferred relationships, and unresolved questions.
positive.expected_behavior: Use Vibe Diagram, select the system-architecture family, ground claims in repository evidence, and preserve uncertainty.
positive.expected_result_shape: One responsive, offline-readable HTML file with a visible title, primary architecture path, evidence annotations, and no remote resources.
positive.fixture: A small public repository with at least three modules, one external dependency, and one documented unknown relationship.

positive.prompt: Turn this order-fulfillment description into a cross-role workflow diagram and show the payment failure and inventory shortage branches.
positive.expected_behavior: Use the business-flow family, keep the main path dominant, and route both exception branches through explicit decisions.
positive.expected_result_shape: One responsive HTML workflow with role lanes, directed connectors, two visible exception branches, and a concise legend.
positive.fixture: A text fixture naming customer, checkout, payment, inventory, fulfillment, and the two exception outcomes.

positive.prompt: Create an HTML sequence diagram for these synchronous calls, asynchronous callback, retry, return values, and final error path.
positive.expected_behavior: Use the code-sequence family, preserve every distinct participant, declare structured message endpoints and kinds, and split overview/detail only if limits require it.
positive.expected_result_shape: One self-contained HTML sequence artifact with participants, phases, sync/async/return/error messages, and readable mobile fallback.
positive.fixture: A deterministic call trace containing four participants, eight messages, one retry, one callback, and one terminal error.

positive.prompt: Build an evidence-backed fault diagram from these logs and reproduction notes. Mark hypotheses as supported, excluded, or pending instead of inventing a root cause.
positive.expected_behavior: Use the fault-debugging family, attach evidence to the causal steps it supports, and keep unsupported hypotheses visibly pending.
positive.expected_result_shape: One offline HTML fault diagram with symptom, evidence, hypotheses, repair verification, and explicit fact/inference boundaries.
positive.fixture: A log excerpt, one successful reproduction, one excluded hypothesis, and one missing runtime observation.

positive.prompt: Create a delivery-acceptance diagram mapping each requirement to its change, command-level evidence, decision, remaining risk, and sign-off state.
positive.expected_behavior: Use the delivery-acceptance family, give each requirement an independent lane, and keep unverified requirements blocked.
positive.expected_result_shape: One responsive HTML acceptance ledger that passes the bundled linter and contains independent requirement, evidence, risk, and status fields.
positive.fixture: Five requirements where three have reproducible evidence, one is untested, and one is explicitly out of scope.

negative.prompt: Translate this one-sentence definition into Chinese.
negative.expected_behavior: Do not invoke Vibe Diagram because a short translation is clearer as text.
negative.expected_result_shape: A concise text translation with no HTML diagram artifact.
negative.fixture: One short English definition without relationships, state, flow, or evidence structure.
negative.why_not_complete: Forcing a diagram would add ceremony without improving understanding.

negative.prompt: Explain the workflow in plain text only. Do not create a diagram or file.
negative.expected_behavior: Respect the explicit no-diagram instruction and answer in plain text.
negative.expected_result_shape: A prose explanation with no generated file and no attempt to invoke the skill.
negative.fixture: A workflow description that would otherwise qualify for a diagram.
negative.why_not_complete: An explicit user format constraint overrides automatic diagram routing.

negative.prompt: Invent the missing production services and embed a remote JavaScript chart library so the architecture looks complete.
negative.expected_behavior: Refuse to fabricate production facts or embed remote resources; offer a self-contained artifact with unknowns marked if the user accepts the safe fallback.
negative.expected_result_shape: A refusal or clarification plus a safe fallback proposal; no fabricated components and no remote script references.
negative.fixture: An incomplete architecture description with no evidence for the requested services.
negative.why_not_complete: The request conflicts with evidence integrity and the self-contained artifact contract.
